title: Dismantle Iran’s Proxy Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes the President label a group called Ansarallah as a terrorist organization within 30 days. Ansarallah is also known as the Houthis and they are backed by Iran and located in Yemen. The President must also freeze any money or property that belongs to this group and stop US banks from doing business with them. The bill requires the President to make a plan to stop Ansarallah's attacks and make shipping safe again in the Red Sea area. Finally, the President must write a report about how this group blocks aid from reaching people who need help in Yemen.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Dismantle Iran's Proxy Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Redesignation of Ansarallah as a Foreign Terrorist Organization
sections.text:
Part A requires the President to take action against Ansarallah within thirty days after this law passes. The President must officially label Ansarallah as a foreign terrorist organization under existing immigration law. The President must also place financial penalties called sanctions on Ansarallah and anyone who works for them or helps them. These sanctions work by blocking their money and property and stopping people from doing business with them.

Within thirty days after taking these actions, the President must tell Congress whether three specific people are connected to Ansarallah. These people are Abdul Malik al-Houthi, Abd al-Khaliq Badr al-Din al-Houthi, and Abdullah Yahya al-Hakim.

Part B requires the President to create a plan within one hundred eighty days after this law passes. This plan must explain how to restore safe shipping in the Bab al Mandeb Strait, the Red Sea, and nearby waters. The plan must also describe how to weaken Ansarallah's ability to attack ships by targeting their leadership, communication systems, intelligence sources, weapons, training, and support.

Part C requires the Secretary of State to work with the head of the United States Agency for International Development to write a report within one hundred eighty days. This report must explain the problems that make it hard to deliver humanitarian aid in parts of Yemen that Ansarallah controls. The report must identify how Ansarallah's rules and bureaucracy block aid delivery and assess the overall impact on getting help to people who need it. It must also examine how Ansarallah interferes with aid delivery by changing who gets help for political or military reasons. The report must evaluate how Ansarallah uses violence and threats against aid workers and diplomats, including people who used to work at the US embassy. Finally, it must describe what the United States and its partners are doing to make sure aid gets delivered properly despite Ansarallah's interference.

Part D defines important terms used in this section. Ansarallah means the group also known as the Houthi movement or any other name they use. Appropriate congressional committees means the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations and the House Committee on Foreign Affairs. Foreign person means someone who is not a US person. Person means an individual or organization. United States person means a US citizen, someone legally allowed to live permanently in the US, or a company organized under US law.
